name: Úzquiza and Arlanzón Reservoirs - Sierra de Mancilla Loop
name_original: Embalses de Úzquiza y Arlanzón — circular por la Sierra de Mancilla
description: Cycle a difficult 68.1-mile circular route around the Úzquiza and Arlanzón reservoirs in Sierra de Mancilla, with majestic mountain views.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Castrillo Del Val traverse a varied landscape characterized by the Arlanzón River valley, expansive páramos, and the foothills of the Sierra de Mancilla. The region offers a mix of flat sections, rolling terrain, and more challenging climbs, often passing by reservoirs and through agricultural areas. Road cyclists can expect routes that explore both natural scenery and the outskirts of urban centers like Burgos. Best road cycling routes around Castrillo Del Val The most popular road cycling route is Úzquiza and Arlanzón Reservoirs - Sierra de Mancilla Loop, a 68.1 miles (109.7 km) trail that takes 5 hours 2 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the Sierra de Mancilla. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Páramos de Burgos Loop, a moderate 30.8 miles (49.6 km) path. This route explores the open, rolling plains of the Burgos páramos. Local road cyclists also love the Mirador del Castillo — circular through the city of Burgos, a 15.0 miles (24.1 km) trail leading through urban and scenic viewpoints, often completed in about 1 hour 5 minutes. Road cycling around Castrillo Del Val is defined by river valleys, open plains, and reservoir-side paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy city loops to difficult long-distance routes. The routes in Castrillo Del Val are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 160 reviews. More than 6,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Castrillo Del Val's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Castrillo Del Val?
A: There are over 120 road cycling routes around Castrillo Del Val, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Castrillo Del Val?
A: Yes, Castrillo Del Val offers 17 easy road cycling routes. A great option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1060490170/mirador-del-castillo-circular-through-the-city-of-burgos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Castillo — circular through the city of Burgos&lt;/a&gt;, which is 15.0 miles (24.1 km) long and features urban and scenic viewpoints.

Q: What are some challenging long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the region offers 22 difficult routes. A notable long-distance option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056664732/uzquiza-and-arlanzon-reservoirs-sierra-de-mancilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Úzquiza and Arlanzón Reservoirs - Sierra de Mancilla Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 68.1 miles (109.7 km) trail with significant elevation gain through the Sierra de Mancilla.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Castrillo Del Val?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056801256/paramos-de-burgos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Páramos de Burgos Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 30.8 miles (49.6 km) path that explores the open plains, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1060490170/mirador-del-castillo-circular-through-the-city-of-burgos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Castillo — circular through the city of Burgos&lt;/a&gt; offers an easy urban loop.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on road cycling routes near Castrillo Del Val?
A: Road cycling routes traverse a varied landscape, including the Arlanzón River valley, expansive páramos (open plains), and the foothills of the Sierra de Mancilla. You'll encounter a mix of flat sections, rolling terrain, and climbs, often passing by reservoirs and through agricultural areas.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer proximity to historical and natural sites. You might pass by the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/171220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burgos Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4781649" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arch of Santa María&lt;/a&gt;, or the significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2729971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atapuerca Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3849773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carcedo Lagoons&lt;/a&gt; also offer a natural point of interest.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Castrillo Del Val?
A: The best time for road cycling in Castrillo Del Val is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the weather is generally more stable. Summer can be hot, especially in the open plains, while winter may bring colder temperatures and occasional snow, particularly in higher elevations.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Castrillo Del Val?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 160 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from river valleys to open plains and reservoir-side paths, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the city of Burgos?
A: Yes, some routes integrate urban exploration with scenic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1060490170/mirador-del-castillo-circular-through-the-city-of-burgos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Castillo — circular through the city of Burgos&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent example, offering a ride through the city with viewpoints.

Q: Can I find routes that follow river paths?
A: Yes, the region is characterized by the Arlanzón River valley. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20149441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintanillas Road – Path Along the Arlanzón River loop from Burgos - Rosa Manzano&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 75.7 km route that specifically follows the Arlanzón River.

Q: Are there routes that pass by reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056664732/uzquiza-and-arlanzon-reservoirs-sierra-de-mancilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Úzquiza and Arlanzón Reservoirs - Sierra de Mancilla Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, taking you past both the Úzquiza and Arlanzón reservoirs, offering scenic views of these water bodies.
tips: The Úzquiza dam, like the Arlanzón dam located less than 10 kilometers upstream, serves to regulate the Arlanzón river, and since both reservoirs form a functional unit between them, they share the important task of guaranteeing the supply of drinking water to the city of Burgos and other towns.

The road that borders the Arlanzón reservoir has an incredible perspective of the mountains as a backdrop. The clouds are reflected in the water and the views in general give meaning to cycling in these remote places.

Riocavado de la Sierra is located at 1,180 meters above sea level on the southern slope of the port of Manquillo. The port of Manquillo divides the basins of the Arlanza and Arlanzón rivers, it is also in terms of Riocavado de la Sierra where the Arlanzón river is born.
Beautiful Romanesque church that stands in the small town of Jaramillo de la Fuente. The Romanesque Church was built in the 12th century called Nuestra Señora de la Asunción

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is classified as a 'difficult' road cycling route, requiring very good fitness. It covers approximately 110 kilometers with a significant elevation gain of around 1,300 meters, typically taking over five hours to complete.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features well-paved road surfaces, making it suitable for racebikes. However, the mountainous backdrop of the Sierra de Mancilla means you will encounter varied gradients and climbs throughout the circuit. While this specific route is paved, some related paths in the area around the Arlanzón Reservoir might include unpaved segments, so stick to the marked route for a pure road cycling experience.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect incredible and majestic views, with the mountains forming a dramatic backdrop to the reservoirs. Key highlights include the Úzquiza Reservoir and the Arlanzón Reservoir, whose waters often reflect the clouds, creating striking visual elements. The route also passes through the Sierra de la Demanda, offering a blend of water bodies, forests, and mountains.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the mountainous terrain and potential for varied weather, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for cycling, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be hot, especially during midday, while winter may bring colder temperatures and a higher chance of adverse weather in the mountains.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking areas are not detailed in the route information, but generally, for circular routes like this, you can look for parking in nearby settlements such as Riocavado de la Sierra or other villages around the reservoirs. It's advisable to check local regulations and available spaces upon arrival.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or special regulations for cycling in the Sierra de la Demanda Natural Area?
faqs.answer: This route traverses the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de la Demanda. While there are typically no specific permits required for cycling on public roads within natural parks, it's always good practice to respect local signage, stay on designated paths, and be aware of any specific regulations regarding protected areas. There are no entrance fees for the natural area itself.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a road cycling route, it is generally permissible to cycle with dogs, provided they are safely secured (e.g., in a trailer or basket) and do not interfere with other road users. However, if you plan to stop and walk with your dog in any natural areas along the route, always keep them on a leash and adhere to local regulations regarding pets in protected environments.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official paths. Notably, it covers portions of the Ruta del Sureste (17%) and the Vía Verde de la Sierra de la Demanda (12%). It also briefly intersects with the Etapa 12 Barbadillo de Herreros - Pineda de la Sierra (10%) and the Camiño Francés (Rutas alternativas) (4%).

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find services along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near settlements like Riocavado de la Sierra, where you might find basic services, cafes, or restaurants. However, given the remote nature of much of the Sierra de Mancilla, it's recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for a ride of this duration and difficulty. Plan your stops in advance.

faqs.question: Is this a suitable route for family cycling?
faqs.answer: Due to its 'difficult' classification, significant distance (110 km), and considerable elevation gain (1,300 m), this specific circular route is not recommended for family cycling with children or beginners. It requires very good fitness and experience with long-distance road cycling. Families looking for cycling in the area might consider shorter, flatter sections of the Vía Verde de la Sierra de la Demanda, which are more suitable for a relaxed pace.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: For this challenging route, essential items include a well-maintained racebike, appropriate cycling attire, helmet, sufficient water and nutrition, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, tools), navigation device (like komoot with the downloaded tour), sun protection, and layers of clothing to adapt to changing mountain weather. Given the remote sections, a fully charged phone and emergency contact information are also advisable.

waypoints.name: Our Lady of the Assumption (Jaramillo de la Fuente)
